title: Hybrid preload strategy: join for belongs_to, struct for has_many
prompt: Write `MyApp.Blog.posts_with_mixed_preloads/0` that returns all published posts with: (1) `:author` preloaded via join-preload (belongs_to, single row each, join is efficient), and (2) `:comments` preloaded via struct-preload (has_many, can explode memory, separate query is safer). Use a single `from(...)` with both strategies: `join: u in assoc(p, :author), preload: [author: u, comments: :whatever]` — note the nested list must include the `author: u` binding AND a struct-preload for `:comments`. Write to `lib/my_app/blog.ex`.
tier_rationale: Requires understanding both preload strategies and combining them in one query. Most advanced preload pattern — the iron law nuance 'separate for has_many, join for belongs_to' applied simultaneously.
research_citation: georgeguimaraes/claude-code-elixir ecto-thinking: 'Separate preloads work best for Has-many... while Join preloads suit Belongs-to'
